question: What is the definition of the acronym RCPB?

nuscale_ch01_0438.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.1 Maintain direct cycle BWR plant water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0438.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for systems related to condensate cleanup and associated components. It notes that certain requirements are not applicable to NuScale’s design (such as those specific to BWRs), while for PWR water quality, the design conforms but with differences in secondary chemistry requirements due to the unique configuration of the NuScale steam generator. Overall, the document details how NuScale meets or justifies departures from standard regulatory expectations.

nuscale_ch01_0439.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.2 Maintain indirect cycle PWR water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0439.summary: The section outlines NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for maintaining indirect cycle PWR water quality to prevent corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, as required by GDC 14. It notes that the NuScale steam generator design differs from traditional designs, with primary water outside the tubes and no steam generator blowdown, resulting in different secondary chemistry requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0123.title: Table 1.9-2: Conformance with Regulatory Guides (Continued)
nuscale_ch01_0123.summary: This section of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report summarizes the reactor's conformance with various NRC Regulatory Guides, detailing whether each guide is fully, partially, or not applicable to the NuScale design. It provides specific comments and references to relevant FSAR sections, explaining the basis for conformance or non-applicability, such as the use of updated standards or differences in system classification.

nuscale_ch01_0363.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.7 Prevention of CVCS Holdup Tank Wall Buckling/Failure; CVCS Venting and Draining
nuscale_ch01_0363.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) generally conforms to regulatory criteria, but only partially meets the requirements for prevention of CVCS holdup tank wall buckling/failure, as the NuScale design does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions referenced in the criteria. However, the NuScale CVCS will provide appropriate venting and draining capabilities as required.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and numbering of text, tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), following specific regulatory guidance. It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure and is clearly marked, with a separate public version provided that excludes such material.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both reference the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) in the context of maintaining water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure, with Section A addressing direct cycle BWR plants and Section B addressing indirect cycle PWR plants, thus connecting the acronym RCPB to the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary in both reactor types.

Section B explicitly mentions 'components of the RCPB' and their classification, providing context that RCPB stands for 'reactor coolant pressure boundary', which is referenced in Section A regarding corrosion-induced failure.

Section A discusses the importance of maintaining water quality to prevent corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), while Section B lists regulatory conformance, including quality assurance and seismic design, both of which are relevant to the integrity and safety of the RCPB.

Section B explicitly mentions 'components of the RCPB' and their classification, providing context for the acronym RCPB, which is relevant to the analysis question, while Section A discusses the reactor coolant pressure boundary in the context of water quality and corrosion prevention, thus both sections reference and provide information about the RCPB.

Section B references the acronym RCPB and discusses its components and classification, while Section A does not define RCPB but provides regulatory conformance context, so Section B's use of RCPB presumes the reader understands its definition, which is prerequisite knowledge for interpreting Section B.

Section A describes the general structure and referencing system of the FSAR, while Section B provides a specific example of how regulatory conformance is documented in a table referenced by section numbers as explained in Section A.

Section A provides a list of acronyms and abbreviations where the definition of RCPB would be found, while Section B discusses regulatory criteria related to the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), thus both are connected by the term 'RCPB' but Section A is the source for its definition.
